sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for this racing game

special_mechanics.name: Car Acceleration and Top Speed
special_mechanics.description: Engine upgrades increase acceleration speed and marginally improve top speed. Gearbox upgrades significantly improve top speed and acceleration (especially from tier 3 onwards). Cars become slower and more difficult to handle than the original game, with more realistic handling.

physics_values.engine_upgrade_effect: faster acceleration, slight top speed increase
physics_values.gearbox_upgrade_effect: significant top speed improvement, acceleration boost from tier 3+
physics_values.handling: more difficult, more realistic than predecessor

special_mechanics.name: Nitro Boost
special_mechanics.description: Players receive 6 nitro boosts at race start. Can be upgraded to accelerate more strongly and reach higher top speeds. Additional boosts can be collected on tracks ('N' pickups). Some tracks have 'S' pickups that provide automatic nitro boost each lap.

physics_values.upgrade_effect: stronger acceleration, higher top speed reach

special_mechanics.name: Fuel Economy
special_mechanics.description: Gearbox upgrades improve fuel economy. Fuel management is critical on longer races in later game portions. Genesis port does not implement fuel gauge.

special_mechanics.name: Tire Grip and Turning
special_mechanics.description: Tire upgrades improve grip and turning performance. Weather conditions (wet/dry) require tire changes during races.

special_mechanics.name: Damage System
special_mechanics.description: Armor sections can be destroyed. If a section is destroyed and the car takes damage in that area, the car will spin out. Armor upgrades allow cars to take more damage before destruction.

physics_values.spinout_trigger: damage to unarmored area after armor destruction

tier_notes: Tier 2: Empirical/Qualitative data. No specific physics values (velocity units, frame counts, sub-pixel precision) are documented in available sources. Game is racing-focused, not character platformer, so standard jump/gravity mechanics do not apply. Documentation focuses on gameplay mechanics (upgrades, fuel, damage) rather than precise movement parameters.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Top Gear 2 (SNES)
